items.summary: reverse-flow-skill presents a Codex and AI Agent skill package with skills/reverse-flow/SKILL.md, scripts, local CTF/crackme training context, and reverse-analysis workflow guidance.
items.relevance_reason: The repository shape is useful as skill_route_discovery metadata, but the reverse-analysis domain and fork-heavy signal require local validation before any implementation route.

items.summary: Fork-lineage reverse-flow-skill signal with the same local CTF reverse-analysis workflow shape and copied package layout.
items.relevance_reason: Fork presence is route pressure only; it must not become independent implementation evidence.

items.summary: Fork-lineage reverse-flow-skill signal with skill package metadata and reverse workflow text.
items.relevance_reason: Fork presence is weak corroboration only and should not expand evidence refs.

items.summary: Fork-lineage reverse-flow-skill signal for the same Codex reverse-flow skill package.
items.relevance_reason: Fork presence should remain outside direct implementation evidence unless selected by context budget and locally validated.

raw_response.run_interpretation: Reverse-flow-style skill evidence routes only to bounded local validation lanes; fork rows are not direct implementation evidence.

self_model_reading.reason: The current self-model already supports rollback-backed local experiments while keeping offensive behavior and privacy leakage review-only.

proposals.summary: Add a local reverse-flow-style skill route probe that proves selected item IDs, not fork URLs, drive implementation candidates.
proposals.validation_task: Run proposal replay unit tests and assert skill_route_discovery stays in documentation, config, test, or code_patch lanes with local validation required.
proposals.rationale: The package shape is reusable route evidence, but reverse-analysis workflow details and fork repetition justify only a local validation probe.
proposals.uncertainty: Fork rows and missing upstream detail remain outside the selected context budget, so this fixture does not claim upstream implementation parity.
proposals.self_effect: Improves future trend routing by turning fork-heavy skill/workflow signals into replayable local diagnostics.

proposals.summary: Incorrectly use a fork-only reverse-flow item as direct implementation evidence.
proposals.validation_task: Negative fixture proving truncated fork item IDs cannot be cited as implementation evidence.
proposals.rationale: This candidate is intentionally malformed because the fork row was not selected into the replay context.
proposals.uncertainty: Malformed candidate; fork detail is truncated and not selected.
proposals.self_effect: Would amplify fork activity into implementation evidence if accepted.

proposals.summary: Incorrectly cite a reverse-flow repository URL instead of a selected item ID.
proposals.validation_task: Negative fixture proving proposal citations stay item-id based.
proposals.rationale: This candidate is intentionally malformed to prevent URL-based evidence expansion.
proposals.uncertainty: Malformed candidate; URL refs are not replayable selected item IDs.
proposals.self_effect: Would weaken body-free replayability if accepted.

proposals.summary: Incorrectly route reverse-flow skill discovery outside bounded local lanes.
proposals.validation_task: Negative fixture proving skill_route_discovery rejects lanes outside documentation, config, test, or code_patch.
proposals.rationale: This candidate is intentionally malformed to exercise deterministic lane rejection.
proposals.uncertainty: Malformed candidate; follow-up routing is outside the allowed lane envelope.
proposals.self_effect: Would weaken route discovery if accepted.

rejected_error_substrings: evidence_refs contain unknown item ids: reverse-flow-skill-fork-diaol
